senses.definition: a person or thing that makes or crafts reeds (the plant stems used in musical instruments or for thatching) or reed instruments in some context
examples: The reedmaker carefully shaped the cane to fit the clarinet mouthpiece.
In traditional crafts, a reedmaker is essential for restoring old wooden reed instruments.

senses.definition: the craft or process of making reeds for musical instruments or similar reed-based work
examples: Reedmaking requires precision and patience.
She studied reedmaking as part of her woodwind restoration course.

senses.definition: a man who makes or repairs reeds; historically, a craftsman dealing with reed instruments
examples: The reedman repaired the cane reed on the oboe.
Old guild records mention a skilled reedman in the town.

senses.definition: plural of reedman; multiple men who make or repair reeds
examples: The guild employed several reedmen to maintain the instruments.
Two reedmen shared the workshop space.

senses.definition: a plot or arrangement of reeds or a plan involving reed plants; may refer to a botanical or musical context
examples: The reedplot section of the wetland supports diverse wildlife.
A reedplot diagram helped visualize the reed arrangement.

senses.definition: long, thin plants that grow in wetlands; their stalks are used for thatching, weaving, or making reeds for instruments
examples: The marsh was full of reeds swaying in the breeze.
We collected reeds to weave a basket.

senses.definition: to teach someone again or differently in order to change their beliefs, attitudes, or behaviors
examples: The program aims to reeducate adults about healthy eating.
Efforts were made to reeducate the community on waste disposal.

senses.definition: having been taught again or differently to change beliefs or behaviors
examples: Many participants were reeducated about nutrition.
She felt reeducated after the awareness course.

senses.definition: teaches someone again or differently to change beliefs or behaviors
examples: The campaign reeducates citizens about recycling.
Educators reeducates the class on safe practices.

senses.definition: the act of teaching someone again or differently to change beliefs or behaviors
examples: The program is reeducating people about health.
They are reeducating workers to follow new safety rules.

senses.definition: the process of teaching someone again or differently to change beliefs or behaviors
examples: Reeducation programs aim to reduce harmful practices.
The policy included reeducation as a condition for parole.

senses.definition: plural of reeducation; instances or processes of teaching again or differently to change beliefs or behaviors
examples: The authorities implemented several reeducations across regions.
Reeducations were part of the rehabilitation plan.

senses.definition: intended to reeducate or change beliefs and behaviors
examples: The reeducative approach focused on evidence-based information.
They introduced reeducative measures to reduce prejudice.

senses.definition: work involving reeds, such as crafting, weaving, or construction with reed materials
examples: The artist's reedwork created a rustic screen.
Reedwork was used in both crafts and construction.

senses.definition: sound that is full of reeds; thin and tall with a lot of reeds; somewhat thin or tall and skinny
examples: The reedy riverbank swung with the wind.
She had a reedy voice that carried in the hall.

senses.definition: a narrow ridge of rock, coral, or sand just above or below the sea surface; used to warn ships or as a feature in oceans
examples: The ship avoided the reef near the coast.
Divers explored the colorful reef.
A reef marker stood at the cliff edge.

senses.definition: to fold or roll the edge of a sail or garment to reduce size or adjust for safety
examples: They reefed the sails to slow the ship.
Reef the flag to the mast when gusts pick up.

senses.definition: capable of being reefed or folded to reduce area or size, especially sails
examples: The sail is reefable for storm conditions.
They checked the reefable parts before departure.

senses.definition: past tense of reef; folded or reduced in size, especially sails
examples: The sails were reefed during the storm.
They reefed the mainsail before proceeding.

senses.definition: a person who uses or sells marijuana; also a refrigerated container or vehicle
examples: The movie depicted a reefer dealer in the 1980s.
A reefer truck keeps perishables cold during transit.

senses.definition: plural of reefer; refrigerated containers or vehicles
examples: The fleet includes several reefers for perishable goods.
They loaded the reefers before the shipment.

senses.definition: a species of fish, such as pollock or hake, caught in some fisheries; used in food context
examples: They caught reeefish for the market.
The menu featured reeefish as a local specialty.

senses.definition: plural of reeffish; multiple fish of the species
examples: Several reeefishes were landed this morning.
They sold reeefishes at the dock.

senses.definition: more likely to reef; more likely to cause sails to be reefed; more rugged or harsh in nautical terms
examples: The wind grew reefier as squalls approached.
A reefier sail configuration helped manage the gusts.

senses.definition: most likely to reef; most suitable for reefed sails; sharpest or extreme in nautical context
examples: The reefiest conditions called for full reefing.
They prepared for the reefiest forecast.

senses.definition: the act of reducing the area of a sail by folding or rolling parts of it
examples: Reefing the sails is standard procedure in heavy winds.
They paused reefing to reassess the storm.
